leadText: Cradily (Japanese: ユレイドル Yuradle) is a dual-type Rock/Grass Fossil Pokémon introduced in Generation III. It evolves from Lileep starting at level 40.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Cradily is a green, plant-like echinoderm Pokémon with various yellow markings on its head, neck, and torso. There are circular, yellow eyespots on its head. However, its true eyes are concealed inside the dark, crescent opening on its head. Surrounding its head at the base are eight pink tentacles, which somewhat resemble flower petals. Its long, flexible neck has yellow bands in the center, and it can be extended to assist in capturing prey. Its body is short and squat with four stubby growths and a yellow, oval, target-like pattern on the chest. Similar to a crinoid , Cradily's heavy body acts as an anchor, preventing it from getting washed away by rough ocean currents. In ancient times, it built its nest in the shallows of warm seas . When the tide went out, it came up on land to search for prey. However, Cradily cannot move very fast due to its short legs. Instead, Cradily can extend its neck and tentacles by three times its usual length. It then uses its tentacles to capture them and melts them with strong digestive acidic fluid secreted by the tentacles. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Cradily evolves from Lileep . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Lileep Rock Grass → First Evolution Cradily Rock Grass

Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation III. Generation III Hoenn #134 Kanto #— Ruby Cradily roams around the ocean floor in search of food. This Pok é mon freely extends its tree trunk-like neck and captures unwary prey using its eight tentacles. Sapphire Cradily 's body serves as an anchor, preventing it from being washed away in rough seas. This Pok é mon secretes a strong digestive fluid from its tentacles. Emerald It drags its heavy body along the seafloor. It makes its nest in the shallows of warm seas. Cradily can be seen on beaches when the tide goes out. FireRed It ensnares prey with its eight tentacles. It then melts the prey with a strong acid before feeding. LeafGreen Generation IV Sinnoh #— Johto #— Diamond It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches. Pearl Platinum HeartGold It lives in warm seas. Its heavy body weighs it down so it won't get washed away in rough weather. SoulSilver Generation V Unova #— Black It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches. White Black 2 It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches. White 2 Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #— Mountain Kalos #— Hoenn #139 X It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches. Y It ensnares prey with its eight tentacles. It then melts the prey with a strong acid before feeding. Omega Ruby Cradily roams around the ocean floor in search of food. This Pokémon freely extends its tree trunk-like neck and captures unwary prey using its eight tentacles. Alpha Sapphire Cradily's body serves as an anchor, preventing it from being washed away in rough seas. This Pokémon secretes a strong digestive fluid from its tentacles. Generation VII Alola US UM : #232 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Sun, Moon , Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Ultra Sun Normally, it lived on shallow sea shoals. When the tide went out, this Pokémon came up on land to search for prey. Ultra Moon This carnivorous Pokémon lived in primordial seas. It catches prey in its eight tentacles and dissolves them with digestive fluid as it eats. Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #— Crown Tundra #184 Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Arceus . Sword It has short legs and can't walk very fast, but its neck and tentacles can extend to over three times their usual length to nab distant prey. Shield Once Cradily catches prey in its tentacles, it digests them whole and absorbs their nutrients. Brilliant Diamond It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches. Shining Pearl Generation IX Paldea #— Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Generation IX . Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry AG102 Cradily May's Pokédex Cradily, the Barnacle Pokémon. Cradily is believed to be able to extend its tree branch-like neck and catch prey with its tentacles. Episode Pokémon Source Entry JN118 Cradily Goh's Rotom Phone Cradily, the Barnacle Pokémon. A Rock and Grass type. After grabbing food with its tentacles, it gains nutrients by digesting the food whole.
